'''Stefan Marsh''' is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|ce}} or {{rlp|wg}}er for the [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. He previously played for the [[Wigan Warriors]] before being released at the end of the 2012 [[Super League]] season, which he spent on loan at Widnes.

==Background==
Marsh was born in [[Wigan, Greater Manchester, England]].{{citation needed|date=February 2020}}

==Career==
===Wigan===
Marsh was signed by the [[Wigan Warriors]] in 2007 from amateur side [[Wigan St Patricks]]. He made his first-grade début at the 2010 [[Magic Weekend]] at [[Murrayfield Stadium|Murrayfield]] in a 28-10 win over [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]].<ref name="Warriors charge past Giants">{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugbyleague/match_report/0,19936,11070_3274221,00.html|title=Warriors charge past Giants|publisher=skysports.com|date=2010-05-02|accessdate=2010-05-03}}</ref><ref name="Official Match Report">{{cite web|url=http://superleague.co.uk/report.php?id=3274221|title=Official Match Report|publisher=superleague.co.uk|date=2010-05-02|accessdate=2010-05-03}}</ref>

===Widnes===
Marsh spent the 2012 season on loan at [[Super League]] club [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]], developing into a specialist centre. After being released by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at the end of the season, he rejoined Widnes on a permanent deal, signing a two-year contract.<ref>{{cite web|title=Widnes Vikings sign Wigan's Stefan Marsh and Joe Mellor|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19897439|website=BBC Sport|access-date=3 August 2014|date=10 October 2012}}</ref>
